mnemonics: Static in Interface: Helper, Not Heart
keyPoints: Static methods in interfaces are called using the interface name itself (e.g., MyInterface.staticMethod()).
They are utility methods that are not tied to any specific instance of a class.
They cannot be overridden by implementing classes, as they are not inherited.
commonMistakes: Trying to override a static method from an interface in an implementing class, which is not allowed.
Attempting to call a static interface method using an instance of a class that implements the interface, which results in a compiler error.
